title: Cascaded Multitask Stop-Horizon Model
summary: A recent pit-stop paper reports a cascaded setup where next-lap classification is complemented by a regression head for laps until stop.
rationale: A recent pit-stop paper reports a cascaded setup where next-lap classification is complemented by a regression head for laps until stop. That auxiliary target matches the competition structure better than pure binary classification because rows close to a stop should share signal.
implementation_hint: Train a second model on future stop horizon for train rows, capped at a small window such as 0-8 laps. Feed its OOF predictions or bucketized horizon classes into the final PitNextLap classifier, or jointly train a simple neural/tabular model with binary plus horizon losses. Compare raw blend vs cascade.
expected_effect: Should improve ordering near the positive boundary by forcing the model to learn distance-to-stop geometry instead of only a hard threshold at one lap.
risk: Horizon labels can be noisy around race end or unusual pit sequences; multitask neural models may underperform strong trees if not regularized carefully.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
